title: Where Are My Keys?
learningGoal: The learner will read a narrative text focusing intensively on spatial prepositions (In/On/Under), locating objects in a sequence, and correctly parsing SVO question structures with prepositions attached.
instructions: Read the story about Amit looking for his lost keys. Notice how the location words (in, on, under) always come BEFORE the thing he is looking at ('in the bag', not 'the bag in'). Answer the 8 questions below.

contentItems.text: Try to visualize the exact location of the keys every time Amit looks somewhere new.

readingText: Today is Monday. I am very late for work. Where are my keys? I look everywhere. First, I look in my bag. The keys are not in the bag. There is an apple in the bag. Next, I look on the table. My books are on the table. My pen is on the table, but my keys are not on the table. I go into the bedroom. I look under the bed. Is there a box under the bed? Yes, there is a large box under the bed. The box is very heavy, but the keys are not under the bed. Now, I am angry. Where are they? I look in the kitchen. My mother is in the kitchen. She is drinking tea. 'Are my keys on the fridge?' I ask. She looks on the fridge. 'No,' she answers. 'They are not on the fridge.' I walk into the living room. My sister is sitting on the sofa. My dog, Max, is sleeping on the floor. Max is a good dog. Then, I see something shiny. It is under Max! The keys are under the dog! I laugh. I take my keys. Now, I have my keys in my hand. I say goodbye to my mother. I go to work. What a busy morning!
textInTargetLanguage: Today is Monday. I am very late for work. Where are my keys? I look everywhere. First, I look in my bag. The keys are not in the bag. There is an apple in the bag. Next, I look on the table. My books are on the table. My pen is on the table, but my keys are not on the table. I go into the bedroom. I look under the bed. Is there a box under the bed? Yes, there is a large box under the bed. The box is very heavy, but the keys are not under the bed. Now, I am angry. Where are they? I look in the kitchen. My mother is in the kitchen. She is drinking tea. 'Are my keys on the fridge?' I ask. She looks on the fridge. 'No,' she answers. 'They are not on the fridge.' I walk into the living room. My sister is sitting on the sofa. My dog, Max, is sleeping on the floor. Max is a good dog. Then, I see something shiny. It is under Max! The keys are under the dog! I laugh. I take my keys. Now, I have my keys in my hand. I say goodbye to my mother. I go to work. What a busy morning!
baseLanguageSupportText: आज सोमवार है। मुझे काम के लिए बहुत देर हो रही है। मेरी चाबियां कहाँ हैं? मैं हर जगह देखता हूँ। सबसे पहले, मैं अपने बैग में (in my bag) देखता हूँ। चाबियां बैग में नहीं हैं। बैग में एक सेब है। अगला, मैं मेज़ पर (on the table) देखता हूँ। मेरी किताबें मेज़ पर हैं। मेरा पेन मेज़ पर है, लेकिन मेरी चाबियां मेज़ पर नहीं हैं। मैं बेडरूम में जाता हूँ। मैं बिस्तर के नीचे (under the bed) देखता हूँ। क्या बिस्तर के नीचे कोई बॉक्स है? हाँ, बिस्तर के नीचे एक बड़ा बॉक्स है। बॉक्स बहुत भारी है, लेकिन चाबियां बिस्तर के नीचे नहीं हैं। अब, मैं गुस्से में हूँ। वे कहाँ हैं? मैं रसोई में (in the kitchen) देखता हूँ। मेरी माँ रसोई में हैं। वह चाय पी रही हैं। 'क्या मेरी चाबियां फ्रिज पर (on the fridge) हैं?' मैं पूछता हूँ। वह फ्रिज पर देखती हैं। 'नहीं,' वह उत्तर देती हैं। 'वे फ्रिज पर नहीं हैं।' मैं लिविंग रूम में चलता हूँ। मेरी बहन सोफ़े पर (on the sofa) बैठी है। मेरा कुत्ता, मैक्स, फर्श पर (on the floor) सो रहा है। मैक्स एक अच्छा कुत्ता है। फिर, मुझे कुछ चमकदार दिखाई देता है। यह मैक्स के नीचे (under Max) है! चाबियां कुत्ते के नीचे हैं! मैं हंसता हूँ। मैं अपनी चाबियां लेता हूँ। अब, मेरी चाबियां मेरे हाथ में (in my hand) हैं। मैं अपनी माँ को अलविदा कहता हूँ। मैं काम पर जाता हूँ। कितनी व्यस्त सुबह है!

exampleSentence.targetText: The apple is in the bag.
exampleSentence.transliteration: द ऐपल इज़ इन द बैग।

exampleSentence.targetText: My pen is on the table.
exampleSentence.transliteration: माई पेन इज़ ऑन द टेबल।

exampleSentence.targetText: A box is under the bed.
exampleSentence.transliteration: अ बॉक्स इज़ अंडर द बेड।
exampleSentence.baseTranslation: एक बॉक्स बिस्तर के नीचे है।

exampleSentence.targetText: My mother is in the kitchen.
exampleSentence.transliteration: माई मदर इज़ इन द किचन।

exampleSentence.targetText: Max is sleeping on the floor.
exampleSentence.transliteration: मैक्स इज़ स्लीपिंग ऑन द फ्लोर।
exampleSentence.baseTranslation: मैक्स फर्श पर सो रहा है।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: First, I look in my bag.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: फर्स्ट, आई लुक इन माई बैग।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: सबसे पहले, मैं अपने बैग में देखता हूँ।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: Are my keys on the fridge?
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: आर माई कीज़ ऑन द फ्रिज?
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: क्या मेरी चाबियां फ्रिज पर हैं?

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: The keys are under the dog!
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: द कीज़ आर अंडर द डॉग!

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: Now, I have my keys in my hand.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: नाउ, आई हैव माई कीज़ इन माई हैंड।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: अब, मेरी चाबियां मेरे हाथ में हैं।

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What does Amit find INSIDE his bag? ('in the bag')
What things were ON the table?
What was located UNDER the bed?
Where is the mother located? (Use a preposition in your answer).
Finally, where were the keys hidden?
True or False: The text says 'The dog under keys are'.

expectedSummaryPoints: The learner can distinguish between container prepositions (in) and surface prepositions (on).
The learner recognizes the syntax of Preposition + Article + Noun.

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You successfully navigated a highly complicated search and rescue mission, perfectly parsing prepositional clues.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep at it! Remember that 'in' means inside of something (like a bag or kitchen). 'On' means touching the top of something (like a table).
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Check your answers about Max the dog. He is sleeping ON the floor, but the keys are UNDER him.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Today is Monday. I am very late for work. Where are my keys... (full transcript provided in text)

hintText: Read the questions carefully. Does it ask what is IN the bed, ON the bed, or UNDER the bed? Prepositions completely change the meaning!
commonMistakes: Thinking the Sister is IN the sofa.
Thinking the Keys are IN the fridge.
